what_it_does: Implements two passes over HTML mockups: the default pass extracts data-ui-ref addresses, joins them with an address registry, reports orphan mismatches, and writes element-map.json; the optional --extract-candidates pass walks the DOM to emit meaningful UI element candidates to candidates.json for downstream registration.
observations: Two distinct CLI modes: default main() and --extract-candidates via _run_extract().
Uses html.parser.HTMLParser in a streaming, manual-stack walk rather than a full DOM library.
Maintains open-tag stack plus per-parent nth-of-type counters to synthesize unique CSS selectors.
Candidate filter distinguishes semantic tags from div/span named containers, skipping decorative classes, fragment classes, and div/span children of controls.
Text is split into own direct text and descendant text so containers can be grounded with their contents without treating wrappers as units.
Void and self-closing tags are handled with special emit paths.
Malformed nesting is tolerated by collapsing orphaned intermediate frames into the matched frame and propagating their text.
Orphan reporting covers both used-but-unregistered and registered-but-unused addresses.
Reads addresses.json and corpus-meta.json; writes candidates.json or element-map.json.
The candidates.json schema and filter rationale are extensively documented in the module docstring as a seam contract.
standouts: The candidates.json seam contract is spelled out in detail inside the module docstring.
A custom streaming HTML parser generates nth-of-type selectors and classifies elements by semantic role, text ownership, and ancestor context.
Dual-pass architecture separates auditing existing address usage from discovering new registration candidates.
conventions: Module-level constants for tag/class sets are prefixed with underscore and declared at the top.
Docstring-first design: the candidates pass contract lives in the module docstring.
Internal helpers are prefixed with underscore; public functions are not.
Path resolution is relative to __file__ using os.path.
JSON is read/written with utf-8 encoding and indent=2 formatting.
addresses.json is tolerated both as {addresses:{...}} and as a bare map.
concerns: outerHTML end positions are approximated with html.find('>', start) or a line/col heuristic, which can be off for unusual tag syntax.
Malformed nesting collapse silently drops candidate frames above the matched end tag, potentially losing elements.
Selector paths are built from the full open stack, not explicitly from <body>, so html/head/body frames may appear.
Hard-coded decorative/fragment class sets must be maintained as the design system evolves.
build_map uses a regex over the whole HTML, so data-ui-ref matches inside comments or scripts would be counted.
notes: A self-contained mockup parser that audits addressable UI elements against a registry and optionally produces a candidate ledger for registration.
questions: What is the exact schema of corpus-meta.json, especially the fourth field used as base_address?
Which downstream tools consume candidates.json and element-map.json?
Are addresses.json dossiers guaranteed to contain region, capabilities, represents, code, and howto fields?
Why does __init__ set convert_charrefs=True while the docstring mentions convert_charrefs=False?
How is the RG9 write-back step intended to stamp the generated selectors onto mockups?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches implementation; the file additionally implements the documented --extract-candidates second pass.
apparent_themes: Addressability as the join key between design mockups and code.
Explicit seam contracts between pipeline stages.
Extend-by-registration: new addressable things are added to addresses.json and carried in mockups.
Mechanical sympathy: streaming parser and manual stack instead of heavy dependencies.
intention_signals: The file tries to automate a bidirectional bridge between UI mockups and a canonical address registry, making every meaningful element traceable to a feature and code location while surfacing mismatches and feeding a registration pipeline with pre-filtered candidate units.
novelty: A custom streaming HTML parser that synthesizes stable nth-of-type selectors and classifies DOM elements by semantic role, text ownership, and ancestor context to produce a registration-ready candidate ledger without relying on a full DOM library.
contribution: Provides a concrete, self-contained mockup-to-address mapping tool with two complementary passes: one that audits existing address usage against the registry, and one that discovers candidate elements for future registration, along with explicit filter documentation and orphan reporting.
summary_for_embedding: Python module that parses HTML mockups to extract data-ui-ref addresses, cross-reference them with an address registry, report orphan mismatches, and optionally emit a JSON ledger of meaningful UI element candidates with nth-of-type selectors and ancestor dossiers.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to keep design mockups and the running application's addressable UI surface synchronized by producing auditable element maps and candidate units for registration.

symbols.does: Retrieves an attribute value by name from an HTMLParser attrs list.
symbols.description: Small helper that scans the list of (name, value) tuples produced by HTMLParser and returns the value for the requested attribute, or None if absent.

symbols.does: Streaming HTML parser that walks one mockup and emits candidate element records.
symbols.description: Subclass of HTMLParser that tracks an open-tag stack, nth-of-type sibling counters, direct and descendant text, and ancestor data-ui-ref addresses, then emits candidate units at element boundaries.

symbols.does: Initializes the extractor with HTML, base address, and registry.
symbols.description: Stores the source HTML, fallback base address, and address map; precomputes line-start offsets for absolute positions; and prepares the open stack and sibling counters.

symbols.does: Converts parser (line, col) into an absolute character offset.
symbols.description: Uses the precomputed line-start index to translate HTMLParser's 1-based line and 0-based column into an absolute index into the source string.

symbols.does: Processes an opening tag and pushes a stack frame.
symbols.description: Lowercases the tag, increments the sibling counter for the current parent, records the tag/class/data-ui-ref/start offset, and pushes a new frame onto the open stack.

symbols.does: Handles self-closing tags by emitting them immediately.
symbols.description: Counts the tag among its siblings, captures its start offset and attributes, finds the closing >, and emits a candidate if the element passes the meaningful filter.

symbols.does: Handles void tags encountered as ordinary start tags.
symbols.description: Similar to handle_startendtag but for void tags such as input; locates the closing > and immediately emits a candidate when meaningful.

symbols.does: Records direct text into the current open frame.
symbols.description: Strips whitespace from character data and appends it to the innermost open frame's own_parts, representing the element's own direct text.

symbols.does: Pops the matching frame and propagates text upward.
symbols.description: Finds the matching open tag, collapses any mismatched intermediate frames into it, pops the frame, propagates its full text to the parent, and emits a candidate if meaningful.

symbols.does: Finds the nearest strictly-above data-ui-ref address.
symbols.description: Walks the open stack upward and returns the first frame's data-ui-ref; falls back to the mockup base_address when no registered ancestor exists.

symbols.does: Builds a tag:nth-of-type path from the root to the current element.
symbols.description: Concatenates each open stack frame's tag and nth-of-type counter with ' > ' separators to produce a unique locator for the candidate.

symbols.does: Checks whether the element is inside a control ancestor.
symbols.description: Returns True if any frame on the open stack is a button, anchor, label, or summary, which causes div/span children to be treated as fragments rather than units.

symbols.does: Applies the documented filter to decide whether an element is a candidate unit.
symbols.description: Keeps elements that already carry data-ui-ref or are semantic tags; accepts named div/span only when they have a class, are not decorative or fragment classes, have no control ancestor, and carry sufficient own direct text.

symbols.does: Builds and appends a candidate record when the filter passes.
symbols.description: Computes own and full text, slices a bounded outerHTML, resolves the ancestor address and dossier, and appends the candidate dictionary to the output list if meaningful.

symbols.does: Runs the extractor across all mockups and assembles candidates.json.
symbols.description: Public function that instantiates _CandidateExtractor for each mockup, fills in mockup_file, and returns the full payload including filter documentation and counts.

symbols.does: CLI entry point for the --extract-candidates pass.
symbols.description: Loads addresses.json, corpus-meta.json, and all HTML mockups; calls extract_candidates; writes candidates.json; and prints counts plus a per-tag rollup.

symbols.does: Default pass that maps mockup addresses to the registry and reports orphans.
symbols.description: Finds every data-ui-ref occurrence in the mockups, joins each with the registry, collects used addresses, and returns elements along with unregistered and unused orphan sets and summary counts.

symbols.does: Default CLI entry point that writes element-map.json.
symbols.description: Loads the address registry and mockups, calls build_map, writes element-map.json, and prints a summary of addressed elements and orphans.
